meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: Master Client Workflows to Finish AI SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS development. Use this expert-led client workflow checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your MVP faster and start earning.
content.answer_block: To finish projects, shift from endless coding to a rigid client-facing workflow. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform abstract ideas into shippable modules. Focus on high-value features, automate the grunt work, and validate your build through constant, iterative deployment cycles.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 90% finished projects—beautiful codebases that never saw a paying customer. You are stuck in the 'infinite iteration' loop, where every feature feels essential and every bug feels like a blocker. The thrill of the build has been replaced by the crushing weight of technical debt and scope creep.

But the gap between a stalled project and a profitable SaaS is not more hours—it is a better workflow. By treating your development process like a professional client project, you force discipline onto your creativity. This checklist is your blueprint to break the cycle, ship your MVP, and finally get your product into the hands of users who are ready to pay.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Strip your project back to the single core feature that solves a burning problem. Use this phase to define the boundary of what 'done' looks like.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements and suggest a 3-feature MVP scope that maximizes user value while minimizing development time. Output as a bulleted list.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'review current directory and identify unnecessary boilerplate features'

steps.title: Architect the Data Model
steps.description: Before writing logic, define the database schema. This prevents mid-build refactoring that kills momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean Prisma schema for a SaaS user auth and subscription model. Ensure it follows RDBMS best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create schema.prisma file based on the suggested architecture'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't waste time on auth or UI kits. Use Antigravity to generate the foundation so you can focus on your unique value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Tailwind CSS, Clerk authentication, and Stripe checkout integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure using the antigravity scaffolded files'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Injection
steps.description: Build one feature at a time using Claude Code to handle the implementation, keeping the context window focused on one deliverable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for the subscription webhook handler. Include error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement the webhook handler and add unit tests'

steps.title: Final Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Automate the path to production. If it's not live, it's not real. Ensure your CI/CD pipeline is ready to push to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated deployment to Vercel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure github actions for auto-deploy on main branch push'

pro_tips: Treat every feature like a client request; if it doesn't solve a user problem, kill it immediately.
Use Claude Code to write tests for your code, not just the code itself; it prevents the 'it works on my machine' syndrome.
Never spend more than 4 hours on a single UI component; if it takes longer, use a pre-built component library.
Timebox your development sessions to 90 minutes; deep work beats long, distracted hours every time.
Ship the 'ugly' version first; revenue is the best feedback mechanism for prioritizing your remaining roadmap.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, stop coding and start marketing. Use the feedback from your first 10 users to define the next sprint, and repeat this workflow cycle.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark. Implement this workflow, kill the scope creep, and get your product to market before the competition does. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
